etymology.description: 面(面) (miàn, "face") refers to the front or surface; 前(前) (qián, "front") indicates a position that is before something.
etymology.interpretation: 面(面) (miàn, "face") signifies the side of something that is outward or forward-facing, i.e., the front aspect. 前(前) (qián, "front") intrinsically means ahead or in position before something. When combined, they intuitively form the notion of 'in front of' by positioning 'face' at 'the front', specifying the area directly before a reference point.

examples.chinese: 他站在镜子面前(他站在鏡子面前)。
examples.english: He stands in front of the mirror.

examples.chinese: 在观众面前表演(在觀眾面前表演)，让我感到紧张(讓我感到緊張)。
examples.pinyin: Zài guānzhòng miànqián biǎoyǎn, ràng wǒ gǎndào jǐnzhāng.
examples.english: Performing in front of an audience makes me nervous.

examples.chinese: 雨后，彩虹出现在我们面前(雨後，彩虹出現在我們面前)。
examples.pinyin: Yǔ hòu, cǎihóng chūxiàn zài wǒmen miànqián.
examples.english: After the rain, a rainbow appeared in front of us.
